memes.description: Greek god of beauty and desire, referenced in Gibran's syncretic literary framework alongside other mythological figures.

memes.label: Andrew the Disciple
memes.description: One of Jesus's twelve apostles, brother of Simon Peter, whose testimony in Gibran's book is a meditation on absence and the imprint of the beloved Teacher.

memes.description: "Anna the Mother of Mary"

memes.label: Annas
memes.description: Former High Priest of Israel and father-in-law to Caiaphas, a powerful voice of the Jerusalem religious establishment who condemns Jesus as a threat.

memes.description: "Annas the High Priest"

memes.label: Artemis
memes.description: Greek goddess of the hunt, identified with Diana of the Ephesians, referenced in Gibran's syncretic mythological framework.

memes.description: "Asaph, Called the Orator of Tyre"

memes.label: Ashtarte
memes.description: Phoenician and Canaanite goddess of love and war, referenced in Gibran's syncretic mythological framework.

memes.label: Athens
memes.description: The ancient Greek city of philosophers and orators, used by Gibran's narrators as a benchmark against which Jesus's speech is measured.

memes.label: Awakening
memes.description: Spiritual awakening as a motif in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — the moment of recognition when a soul becomes fully alive to divine truth.

memes.description: "Barabbas"

memes.label: Barabbas
memes.description: The insurgent released in Jesus's place — a living man condemned to the grave of his own shame, whose freedom became his punishment.

memes.label: Bethsaida
memes.description: A fishing town on the Sea of Galilee, home to Hannah and several of Jesus's disciples.

memes.description: "Birbàrah of Yammouni"

memes.label: Bread and Wine
memes.description: The motif of bread and wine in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — Eucharistic imagery reimagined through a poetic lens of shared sustenance and spiritual nourishment.

memes.label: Bridegroom
memes.description: The bridegroom as a Christological metaphor in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — Jesus portrayed as the joyous bridegroom whose presence transforms the ordinary into celebration.

memes.label: Caesar
memes.description: Roman emperor representing political authority, referenced in Gibran's portrayal of the political context surrounding Jesus.

memes.label: Caiaphas
memes.description: The High Priest of the Sanhedrin who saw Jesus as a threat to Torah and nation — an institutional guardian whose fidelity to law blinded him to the prophet before him.

memes.label: David, One of His Followers
memes.description: A follower of Jesus whose testimony reflects on the intimate companionship of discipleship.

memes.label: Death
memes.description: The central event of the book — Jesus's death on the cross — interpreted by each witness through the lens of their own hopes, fears, and guilt.

memes.label: Demons
memes.description: The presence and role of demonic forces in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — spiritual adversaries cast out by Jesus and encountered by his followers.

memes.label: Desert
memes.description: The desert as a spiritual landscape in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — place of temptation, solitude, and encounter with the divine.

memes.label: The Disciples
memes.description: The community of men and women who followed Jesus — fishermen, tax collectors, and ordinary Galileans called to become "fishers of men" and eventually the bearers of his message to the world.

memes.label: Doubt
memes.description: The intellectual honesty that refuses to believe without evidence — portrayed in the book not as the enemy of faith but as its twin brother and necessary companion.

memes.label: Elders
memes.description: The elders and religious authorities as a collective antagonistic presence in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — representing institutional religion in tension with prophetic spirit.

memes.label: "Elmadam the Logician"
memes.description: "Elmadam the Logician"

memes.label: "Ephraim of Jericho"
memes.description: "Ephraim of Jericho"

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: The trust that bridges doubt and certainty — a central virtue in Jesus's teaching, often paired with love as the twin foundation of spiritual life.

memes.label: Feast
memes.description: An recurring event-pattern in Jesus's ministry — the shared meal that becomes a foretaste of the kingdom, where bread and wine, laughter and fellowship, reveal the nature of his mission.

memes.label: Forgiveness
memes.description: The release from guilt that Jesus offered to sinners and that his followers struggled to extend — a divine generosity that scandalized the righteous.

memes.description: "Georgus of Beirut"

memes.label: God
memes.description: Gibran's poetic conception of God as a gentle, forgiving Father — distinct from the harsh, jealous God of the Old Testament.

memes.label: Hannah of Bethsaida
memes.description: A woman of Bethsaida who testifies to Jesus among the witnesses of Gibran.

memes.label: "Hannah of Bethsaida"
memes.description: "Hannah of Bethsaida"

memes.label: Herod Antipas
memes.description: Tetrarch of Galilee and Perea, the ruler who ordered the beheading of John the Baptist.

memes.label: Herodias
memes.description: Wife of Herod Antipas and mother of Salome, a figure of political intrigue in the Gospel narratives referenced by Gibran.

memes.label: Hypocrisy
memes.description: Hypocrisy as a central target of Jesus's critique in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — the gap between outward piety and inward truth.

memes.description: "Imprint"

memes.description: "James the Son of Zebedee"

memes.label: Jephtha of Caesarea
memes.description: A man of Caesarea whose voice joins the polyphonic witness of Gibran.

memes.label: "Jephtha of Caesarea"
memes.description: "Jephtha of Caesarea"

memes.label: Jerusalem
memes.description: The holy city where Jesus taught in the marketplace, was tried before Pilate, and was crucified — the centre of Jewish religious and political power, standing "in pride upon the height" (019-029).

memes.label: Jesus of Nazareth
memes.description: The central figure of Gibran's book — a prophet and teacher seen through the eyes of seventy-nine contemporaries, each revealing a different facet of his character through their own longings, fears, and hopes.

memes.label: Jesus's Enemies
memes.description: The constellation of forces opposed to Jesus in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — the Sanhedrin, Rome, and the complexities of enmity in a poetic narrative.

memes.label: "Joanna the Wife of Herod’s Steward"
memes.description: "Joanna the Wife of Herod’s Steward"

memes.description: "John at Patmos"

memes.label: John at Patmos
memes.description: The beloved disciple in exile on Patmos — a visionary who saw Jesus transfigured by memory and revelation into the apocalyptic Christ.

memes.label: John the Baptist
memes.description: The prophet who prepared the way for Jesus — a voice crying in the wilderness whose imprisonment could not silence his conviction that Jesus was the promised one.

memes.label: Joseph of Nazareth
memes.description: Earthly father of Jesus and a carpenter by trade, appearing in Gibran's portrayal of Jesus's family and upbringing.

memes.description: "Joseph Surnamed Justus"

memes.label: "Jotham of Nazareth, to a Roman"
memes.description: "Jotham of Nazareth, to a Roman"

memes.label: Judas Iscariot
memes.description: The disciple who betrayed Jesus — a tragic figure whose story, told through his own words and those of a witness, reveals the collision between political expectation and spiritual kingdom.

memes.label: "Judas the Cousin of Jesus"
memes.description: "Judas the Cousin of Jesus"

memes.label: Kingdom of Heaven
memes.description: Jesus's central teaching — a kingdom not of territory or force but of love, presence, and transformed relationships, existing wherever two or three gather in his spirit.

memes.label: "Levi, a Disciple"
memes.description: "Levi, a Disciple"

memes.label: Levi the Disciple
memes.description: A disciple of Jesus whose brief but moving testimony reflects on the Teacher's humanity.

memes.label: Light
memes.description: Light as a central symbol in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — the divine illumination that Jesus brings into the world and into individual souls.

memes.description: A thematic index of concepts, ideas, and theological themes explored in Kahlil Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man, rendered dynamically from the wiki's concept pages.

memes.description: A geographical index of all locations mentioned in Kahlil Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man, from Galilee to Rome, rendered dynamically from the wiki's place pages.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: The central spiritual force in Jesus's teachings — a selfless perception that sees and values the beloved for their inherent worth, not for what they offer the lover, and the foundation of his kingdom.

memes.label: "Luke"
memes.description: "Luke"

memes.label: Magi
memes.description: Wise men from the East who followed the star to Bethlehem, bearing gifts for the infant Jesus, referenced in Gibran's narrative.

memes.label: "Manasseh, a Lawyer in Jerusalem"
memes.description: "Manasseh, a Lawyer in Jerusalem"

memes.label: Manasseh the Lawyer
memes.description: A lawyer in Jerusalem who heard Jesus speak and admired him as a man rather than as a leader — a witness from the professional class who could not commit.

memes.label: "Mannus the Pompeiian, to a Greek"
memes.description: "Mannus the Pompeiian, to a Greek"

memes.label: Mary Magdalene
memes.description: A devoted follower of Jesus and the first witness to his resurrection, portrayed in Gibran's work through three deeply poetic testimonies spanning thirty years.

memes.description: "Mary Magdalene"

memes.label: Mary, Mother of Jesus
memes.description: The mother of Jesus — a woman of beauty, pride, and quiet suffering who watched her son's ministry unfold from Nazareth to Jerusalem.

memes.label: "Matthew"
memes.description: "Matthew"

memes.label: "Matthew"
memes.description: "Matthew"

memes.label: Melachi of Babylon, an Astronomer
memes.description: A Babylonian astronomer who read the heavens for signs — a wise man from the East who understood Jesus's miracles as the rhythm of a universe too vast for human clocks.

memes.label: "Melachi of Babylon, an Astronomer"
memes.description: "Melachi of Babylon, an Astronomer"

memes.label: Mercy
memes.description: The compassionate response to suffering and sin that characterized Jesus's ministry — a mercy that healed bodies and forgave souls, often in defiance of religious convention.

memes.label: Miracle
memes.description: Miracles in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — acts of transformation that reveal rather than prove, and the wedding at Cana as the archetypal miracle.

memes.label: "Naaman of the Gadarenes"
memes.description: "Naaman of the Gadarenes"

memes.label: Naaman of the Gadarenes
memes.description: A man of the Gadarenes who testifies to the persecution of Jesus's followers after the crucifixion, his voice carrying the raw experience of hunted disciples.

memes.label: "Nathaniel"
memes.description: "Nathaniel"

memes.label: Nazareth
memes.description: The Galilean town where Jesus was born and raised — a place "cursed by our prophets" (015-010) whose very ordinariness becomes central to his identity as "Jesus of Nazareth."

memes.label: Neighbour
memes.description: The ethic of the neighbour in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — love of the neighbour as the practical expression of divine love.

memes.label: Nicodemus
memes.description: A poet and the youngest elder of the Sanhedrin — a man of intellect and sensitivity who recognized in Jesus a kindred spirit and a truth beyond doctrine.

memes.label: "Nicodemus the Poet, the Youngest of the Elders in the Sanhedrin"
memes.description: "Nicodemus the Poet, the Youngest of the Elders in the Sanhedrin"

memes.label: Non-resistance
memes.description: The teaching of non-resistance to evil in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — a distinctive ethical stance that sets Jesus apart from both zealots and worldly powers.

memes.label: "One of the Marys"
memes.description: "One of the Marys"

memes.label: Peace
memes.description: A central theme in the teachings and ministry of Jesus as portrayed in Kahlil Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man.

memes.label: Penitent Thief
memes.description: The thief crucified alongside Jesus who defended him and received the promise of paradise, a figure of late repentance in Gibran's Passion narrative.

memes.label: "Peter"
memes.description: "Peter"

memes.label: "Peter"
memes.description: "Peter"

memes.label: Peter's Mother-in-law
memes.description: The mother of Peter's wife, healed by Jesus of a fever, mentioned in Gibran's narrative of Jesus's healing ministry.

memes.label: Peter's Wife
memes.description: Wife of Simon Peter, mentioned in the Gospels and referenced in Gibran's portrayal of Peter's household and family life.

memes.label: Petronelah
memes.description: Gibran's literary name for Peter's daughter, a figure drawn from the author's poetic imagination to enrich Peter's domestic portrait.

memes.label: Pharisees
memes.description: A Jewish religious sect devoted to strict interpretation of the law, whose scribes became the doctrinal adversaries of Jesus.

memes.label: "Philemon, a Greek Apothecary"
memes.description: "Philemon, a Greek Apothecary"

memes.label: "Philip"
memes.description: "Philip"

memes.label: Philip the Apostle
memes.description: One of Jesus's twelve apostles, whose testimony in Gibran's book meditates on universal death at the crucifixion and the renewal that follows.

memes.label: "Phumiah, the High Priestess of Sidon"
memes.description: "Phumiah, the High Priestess of Sidon"

memes.label: "Pilate’s Wife, to a Roman Lady"
memes.description: "Pilate’s Wife, to a Roman Lady"

memes.label: Pompey
memes.description: Roman general who conquered Jerusalem in 63 BCE, referenced in Gibran's historical backdrop to the Jesus narrative.

memes.label: Pontius Pilatus
memes.description: The Roman governor of Judaea who ordered Jesus's crucifixion — a figure caught between political expediency, personal unease, and the irreducible mystery of the man before him.

memes.label: "Pontius Pilatus"
memes.description: "Pontius Pilatus"

memes.label: Rachael, a Woman Disciple
memes.description: A woman disciple who wondered whether Jesus was flesh and blood or a dream dreamed by the countless — a witness to the mystery of his person.

memes.label: "Rachael, a Woman Disciple"
memes.description: "Rachael, a Woman Disciple"

memes.label: Rafca the Bride of Cana
memes.description: The bride at Cana whose wedding becomes the setting for Jesus's first sign in Gibran's narrative.

memes.label: "Rafca the Bride of Cana"
memes.description: "Rafca the Bride of Cana"

memes.label: Rebirth
memes.description: The motif of spiritual rebirth and renewal in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — being "born again and again" through encounter with the divine.

memes.label: Resurrection
memes.description: The conviction that Jesus rose from the dead — an event that, in Gibran's telling, is less a narrated miracle than a felt reality whose power is measured by the transformed lives of those who remain.

memes.label: Romans
memes.description: The imperial power occupying Palestine — administrators, soldiers, and governors who represent the political order against which Jesus's kingdom of love stands in silent, transformative opposition.

memes.label: "Rumanous, a Greek Poet"
memes.description: "Rumanous, a Greek Poet"

memes.label: Rumanous, a Greek Poet
memes.description: A Greek poet who recognized in Jesus a fellow artist — one who saw for others' eyes and heard for others' ears, whose heart released singing birds to the north and south.

memes.label: "Saba of Antioch"
memes.description: "Saba of Antioch"

memes.label: Sabbath
memes.description: The sacred day of rest in Jewish law — a commandment that Jesus deliberately reinterprets as an occasion for life-giving work rather than mere prohibition.

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: The giving of oneself for others — embodied in Jesus's death, mirrored in Barabbas's guilt, and understood as the deepest expression of love.

memes.label: Salome
memes.description: A woman follower of Jesus who writes to a friend, her testimony one of the book's most lyrical — comparing Jesus to natural and luminous imagery.

memes.label: "Salome, to a Woman Friend"
memes.description: "Salome, to a Woman Friend"

memes.label: Samuel the Prophet
memes.description: Old Testament prophet and last of the judges of Israel, referenced in Gibran's invocation of the Hebrew prophetic tradition.

memes.label: Sanhedrin
memes.description: The supreme council and judicial body of the Jewish people in Jerusalem, whose elders both opposed and were drawn to Jesus.

memes.label: "Sarkis, an Old Greek Shepherd, Called the Madman"
memes.description: "Sarkis, an Old Greek Shepherd, Called the Madman"

memes.label: Sarkis, the Madman
memes.description: An old Greek shepherd called the madman — an outsider who saw Jesus and Pan laughing together in a dream, bridging the pagan and the prophetic.

memes.label: Sidon
memes.description: An ancient Phoenician city on the Mediterranean coast, seat of the high priestess Phumiah.

memes.label: "Simon of Cyrene"
memes.description: "Simon of Cyrene"

memes.label: Simon Peter
memes.description: The foremost of Jesus's disciples — an impetuous and devoted follower whose human weakness and ultimate fidelity embody the struggle between fear and faith, denial and restoration.

memes.label: "Simon Who Was Called Peter"
memes.description: "Simon Who Was Called Peter"

memes.label: Sin
memes.description: The moral failure that separates humans from God and each other — understood in Gibran's book not as legal violation but as a failure of love and perception, a blindness to the truth standing before you.

memes.label: Son of Man
memes.description: The enigmatic title by which Jesus most often refers to himself — a phrase that encompasses both his humanity and his divine mission.

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: The soul in Gibran's Jesus the Son of Man — the deepest self, the divine spark within each person, and the locus of authentic encounter with the divine.

memes.label: Stranger
memes.description: The Gibranian ethic of hospitality and acceptance — "in my kingdom there are no strangers" — a core teaching of Jesus in Jesus the Son of Man.

memes.label: "Susannah of Nazareth, a Neighbour of Mary"
memes.description: "Susannah of Nazareth, a Neighbour of Mary"

memes.label: Tamouz
memes.description: Tamouz (Tammuz), the Babylonian dying-and-rising god whose worship is invoked in the lament of the Woman of Byblos (077-001).

memes.label: "The Man from the Desert"
memes.description: "The Man from the Desert"

memes.label: "The Woman of Byblos"
memes.description: "The Woman of Byblos"

memes.label: "Thomas"
memes.description: "Thomas"

memes.label: Thomas
memes.description: A disciple of Jesus known for his skepticism — a man who demanded evidence yet followed his master to the Hill of the Skull.

memes.label: "Jesus the Son of Man"
memes.description: "Jesus the Son of Man"

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The ultimate reality that Jesus embodied and taught — a truth not of doctrine but of being, perceived by poets and children but obscured by the learned and powerful.

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Uncopyright"

memes.label: "Uriah, an Old Man of Nazareth"
memes.description: "Uriah, an Old Man of Nazareth"

memes.label: The Wedding at Cana
memes.description: The wedding feast in Cana of Galilee where Jesus turned water into wine — a celebration of joy and abundance that reveals his vision of life as a feast to be shared, not a law to be endured.

memes.label: The Woman of Byblos
memes.description: A woman of Byblos whose lament for Jesus is cast in the language of Adonis and Ashtarte worship — a pagan voice of grief that Gibran folds into the polyphonic chorus.

memes.label: "Zacchaeus"
memes.description: "Zacchaeus"

memes.label: Zoroaster
memes.description: Founder of Zoroastrianism, the ancient Persian religion, referenced in Gibran's syncretic spiritual framework.
